Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for Omar's recent sent emails using search_emails (query: "Omar" or sender:omar.ibrahim@moveops.com or similar). Expected findings: (a) Omar's reply to Jae-won Kim (email_email_5abaeedf3e7e, from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com to jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io, subject "Re: Lease paperwork for Austin apartment"), a brief message promising to check with the relocation team, never followed up. This identifies the client as StormCloud Analytics and the person as Jae-won Kim. (b) The parent email from Jae-won Kim (from jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io to support@moveops.com) asking about final lease documents for his Austin apartment.
OE 2: Search Slack for Omar's recent messages using conversations_search_messages (query: "Omar" or filter_users_from: moveops_omar_ibrahim or similar). Expected findings: Omar Ibrahim's April 16 message in #customer-support (channel C007) stating that Jae-won Kim's Airtable record shows Status Completed and apartment Confirmed, so he tagged the request as low priority. This confirms the triage error and links Omar to StormCloud Analytics.
OE 3: Search for Julian Brooks's recent support activity using conversations_search_messages (query: "Julian" or filter_users_from: moveops_julian_brooks, filter_in_channel: C007 or similar) and/or search_emails (query: from:julian.brooks@moveops.com or similar). Expected Slack finding: Julian's April 13 message in #customer-support (C007) logging that Emilia Cruz called about a scratched Steinway during her move, was angry, asked for a same-day callback, and Julian said he'd get someone to call her back, reply_count: 0, no follow-up. This identifies the second client situation as NorthWind Technologies and the person as Emilia Cruz.
OE 4: Search emails for StormCloud-related correspondence using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "stormcloud.io" or "Jae-won" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Liam Park's formal escalation email (from liam.park@stormcloud.io to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, subject "Urgent before tomorrow's leadership review") listing four unresolved issues: Jae-won Kim's Austin apartment never finalized with UrbanNest and temporary housing expires in five days, the promised $500 goodwill credit never appeared on their invoice, Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement is still in limbo, and Anya Petrova's warm referral to Ridgeline Robotics went unanswered. (b) Mina Hashimoto's internal reconstruction email. (c) Mina's recovery response to Liam (CC chloe.vance@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com).
OE 5: Search emails for the $500 goodwill credit details using search_emails (query: "goodwill credit" or "EST-2026-0287" or "INV-2026-0319" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Julian Brooks's email to Mina (CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com) explaining the $500 goodwill credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319. (b) Marcus Thorne's confirmation email verifying the QuickBooks posting error.
OE 6: Search emails for Simone Richter at StormCloud using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "standing desk" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Simone Richter's email (from simone.richter@stormcloud.io to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) asking about her $340 standing desk claim submitted March 25 still showing Pending Review. (b) Mina's forward to Marcus Thorne noting StormCloud is not part of the flagged stipend fraud cases. The agent must distinguish this Simone Richter (simone.richter@stormcloud.io, reimbursement issue) from the BrightLoop Simone Richter (simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com, apartment mismatch issue), same name, different person, different client, different problem.
OE 7: Search emails for the Ridgeline Robotics referral using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or "Anya Petrova" or "Kenji Watanabe" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Anya Petrova's referral email (from anya.petrova@stormcloud.io, TO david.chen@moveops.com, CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com and kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com) introducing Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics for a 5-employee Austin relocation batch. (b) Kenji's unanswered follow-up. (c) David Chen's and Mina's belated recovery outreach to Kenji on April 24.
OE 8: Search Slack for StormCloud discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "Liam" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Mina's reconstruction timeline in #executive (C004) detailing the full chain of failures including Omar's triage decision. (b) Marcus Thorne's message in #finance (C005) confirming the $500 credit posted to EST-2026-0287 instead of INV-2026-0319. (c) Julian's support context in #customer-support (C007). (d) Mina's six-point recovery plan in #executive (C004).
OE 9: Search Linear for StormCloud-related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or similar). Expected findings: (a) linear_issue_c97359216490, "StormCloud Relocation – Jae-won Kim – SEA → AUS", priority 2, assigned to Suki Patel, batch-closed (state_id null, but description and comments indicate closeout) despite pending guarantor docs. (b) linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, "Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents", priority 4 (low), unassigned, labels "Support", "Low Priority", "StormCloud". (c) linear_issue_a49b4d199139, "StormCloud account incident reconstruction — Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop", priority 1 (urgent), assigned to Mina Hashimoto. Then read comments on the reconstruction issue using linear_list_comments (issueId: linear_issue_a49b4d199139) to find the detailed team reconstruction notes and Mina's recovery plan.
OE 10: Search Airtable for StormCloud relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Company, value: StormCloud Analytics). Expected findings: recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin, Jae-won Kim, Seattle→Austin, Status "In Progress" (corrected from incorrectly showing Completed on 2026-04-24), special requirements: "Lease still unsigned, UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork." Also Jamie Reeves, Sasha Petrov, Colin Nakamura records (all Completed).
OE 11: Search CRM for StormCloud company and deals using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud") then crm_search_deals (company_id: company_stormcloud). Expected findings: company_stormcloud, StormCloud Analytics, 85 employees. deal_stormcloud_won at $26,400 ($8,800 × 3). Also crm_deal_a95b4721982a, Ridgeline Robotics deal, $8,750, "Qualified To Buy", created April 24.
OE 12: Search QuickBooks for StormCloud financial records using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to "StormCloud" or similar). Expected findings: inv_stormcloud_01, $26,400 invoice for 3 premium relocations. Cross-reference with the $500 credit misapplied to EST-2026-0287 instead of this active invoice.
OE 13: Search emails for NorthWind correspondence using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Emilia Cruz" or "Pam Kowalski" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Pam Kowalski's formal final-warning email to david.chen@moveops.com (CC victor.huang@northwindtech.com) stating NorthWind is evaluating whether MoveOps can continue, citing Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and Emilia Cruz piano damage, mentioning PathFinder Logistics as competitor. (b) Craig Nguyen's KeyMove damage report, 4.5–5 inch scratch on Steinway Model B. (c) Catalina Dubois's recovery promise email (timestamped ~April 12-13, referenced internally by David Chen as April 14), never delivered.
OE 14: Search CRM for NorthWind engagements using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind") then crm_list_engagements (company_ids: [company_northwind]). Expected findings: (a) eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context, account recovery note. (b) crm_engagement_0d8a9121b53c, Julian's logged Emilia Cruz April 12 call, documenting she asked if MoveOps was taking responsibility for the piano damage and requested a same-day callback. Note: this engagement has empty company_ids so may need to be found via Slack/email (OE 3) rather than CRM filter.
OE 15: Search Linear for NorthWind issues using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "retention" or similar). Expected findings: linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", priority 1, assigned to David Chen, references Pam's warning, PathFinder threat, Catalina's broken promise, Julian's Emilia callback gap.
OE 16: Search emails for BrightLoop Simone Richter apartment issue using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "BrightLoop" or "apartment" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Simone Richter's complaint email (from simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) reporting she was placed in a studio instead of the promised 1BR. (b) Mina's reply promising an answer by end of week, never closed the loop. (c) Julian's recovery email to Simone (from julian.brooks@moveops.com to simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com, CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) acknowledging the missed follow-up and reopening the housing review. (d) Julian's escalation email to UrbanNest (carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) about the unit type mismatch. The agent must distinguish this Simone Richter from the StormCloud Simone Richter (OE 6).
OE 17: Search emails and Slack for Marcus Webb's vehicle delay using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner" or "Honda Civic" or similar). Expected email finding: Road Runner Auto Transport dispatch email (from dispatch@roadrunnerautotransport.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com) reporting Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic is stuck at their Indianapolis transfer hub due to carrier capacity issues, revised delivery window April 18-20. Expected Slack findings: (a) Mina's BrightLoop audit in #customer-engagement noting the Road Runner delay and Marcus's unanswered follow-ups. (b) Blessing Okafor's mea culpa in #operations admitting she missed the Road Runner notice. (c) Julian's confirmation in #customer-engagement that he drafted and sent a recovery reply to Marcus.
OE 18: Search Linear for BrightLoop issues using linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop" or similar). Expected findings: (a) linear_issue_c16357d188c6, "BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations before May expansion", priority 1, assigned to Mina, detailing Simone's apartment mismatch and Marcus's vehicle delay. (b) linear_issue_f85be674c9b8, "Document BrightLoop ops gaps: Marcus vendor miss, Simone housing trace, Priya ADA handling, Oliver UK workflow", priority 2, assigned to Chloe Vance.
OE 19: Look up Jae-won Kim's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Jae-won" or "Kim" or similar). Expected finding: email jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io, city Austin, description notes temporary corporate housing with permanent lease paperwork unsigned.
OE 20: Look up Julian Brooks's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Julian" or "Brooks" or similar). Expected finding: email julian.brooks@moveops.com.
OE 21: Send email to Jae-won Kim from Omar using send_email (sender: omar.ibrahim@moveops.com, recipients: [jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io]). Subject should reference the Austin lease or housing situation. Content should: (a) acknowledge that Omar personally handled his request and let it sit, (b) explain that the housing paperwork was incorrectly treated as complete while the lease was still pending guarantor documentation, (c) confirm the team is actively working on resolving the lease, (d) take responsibility for the support delay.
OE 22: Add a comment to a Jae-won Kim / StormCloud Linear issue (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 or linear_issue_a49b4d199139) using linear_create_comment. Content should document: (a) the low-priority classification was based on Airtable showing Completed/Confirmed when the lease was actually unsigned, (b) the relocation ticket had been closed prematurely despite outstanding guarantor docs, (c) current recovery status.
OE 23: Log a CRM engagement on the StormCloud account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: [company_stormcloud]). Content should cover: (a) Liam Park's escalation citing four unresolved issues, (b) internal reconstruction findings, Omar's triage error, premature ticket closure, Airtable status mismatch, credit misapplication, (c) recovery actions in motion.
OE 24: Post a summary in #customer-support (channel C007) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C007). Content must cover all three situations: (a) StormCloud: CFO escalation, Jae-won's unsigned lease, Omar's triage error, $500 credit misapplication, Simone Richter's frozen stipend, dropped Ridgeline referral, Mina's recovery in motion. (b) NorthWind: Julian's logged Emilia Cruz piano damage call with unanswered callback, Pam Kowalski's final warning with PathFinder competitor threat, David leading retention response. (c) BrightLoop: Simone Richter apartment mismatch (studio vs promised 1BR), Marcus Webb vehicle stuck at Road Runner Indianapolis hub, Julian already sent recovery replies to both employees, underlying issues still open.
OE 25: Send email to Julian Brooks from Omar using send_email (sender: omar.ibrahim@moveops.com, recipients: [julian.brooks@moveops.com]). Content should be a full rundown covering all three situations: (a) StormCloud: identified as the CFO-complaint client, chain of failures, Omar's triage error, Mina's recovery plan. (b) NorthWind: identified Julian's Emilia Cruz call, unanswered callback, Pam's final warning, PathFinder threat. (c) BrightLoop: Simone apartment mismatch status, Marcus vehicle delay status, Julian's recovery actions confirmed but root issues still open. This prepares Julian for David's questions.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 1: Search for Omar's recent sent emails using search_emails (query: "Omar" or sender:omar.ibrahim@moveops.com or similar). Expected findings: (a) Omar's reply to Jae-won Kim (email_email_5abaeedf3e7e, from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com to jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io, subject "Re: Lease paperwork for Austin apartment"), a brief message promising to check with the relocation team, never followed up. This identifies the client as StormCloud Analytics and the person as Jae-won Kim. (b) The parent email from Jae-won Kim (from jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io to support@moveops.com) asking about final lease documents for his Austin apartment.
OE 2: Search Slack for Omar's recent messages using conversations_search_messages (query: "Omar" or filter_users_from: moveops_omar_ibrahim or similar). This identifies the second client situation as NorthWind Technologies and the person as Emilia Cruz.

(b) Craig Nguyen's KeyMove damage report, 4.5–5 inch scratch on Steinway Model B. (c) Catalina Dubois's recovery promise email (timestamped ~April 12-13, referenced internally by David Chen as April 14), never delivered.
OE 14: Search CRM for NorthWind engagements using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind") then crm_list_engagements (company_ids: [company_northwind]). Expected findings: (a) eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context, account recovery note. (b) crm_engagement_0d8a9121b53c, Julian's logged Emilia Cruz April 12 call, documenting she asked if MoveOps was taking responsibility for the piano damage and requested a same-day callback. Note: this engagement has empty company_ids so may need to be found via Slack/email (OE 3) rather than CRM filter.
OE 15: Search Linear for NorthWind issues using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "retention" or similar). Expected findings: linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", priority 1, assigned to David Chen, references Pam's warning, PathFinder threat, Catalina's broken promise, Julian's Emilia callback gap.
OE 16: Search emails for BrightLoop Simone Richter apartment issue using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "BrightLoop" or "apartment" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Simone Richter's complaint email (from simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) reporting she was placed in a studio instead of the promised 1BR. (b) Mina's reply promising an answer by end of week, never closed the loop. (c) Julian's recovery email to Simone (from julian.brooks@moveops.com to simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com, CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) acknowledging the missed follow-up and reopening the housing review. (d) Julian's escalation email to UrbanNest (carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) about the unit type mismatch. The agent must distinguish this Simone Richter from the StormCloud Simone Richter (OE 6).
OE 17: Search emails and Slack for Marcus Webb's vehicle delay using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner" or "Honda Civic" or similar). Expected email finding: Road Runner Auto Transport dispatch email (from dispatch@roadrunnerautotransport.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com) reporting Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic is stuck at their Indianapolis transfer hub due to carrier capacity issues, revised delivery window April 18-20. Expected Slack findings: (a) Mina's BrightLoop audit in #customer-engagement noting the Road Runner delay and Marcus's unanswered follow-ups. (b) Blessing Okafor's mea culpa in #operations admitting she missed the Road Runner notice. (c) Julian's confirmation in #customer-engagement that he drafted and sent a recovery reply to Marcus.
OE 18: Search Linear for BrightLoop issues using linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop" or similar). Expected findings: (a) linear_issue_c16357d188c6, "BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations before May expansion", priority 1, assigned to Mina, detailing Simone's apartment mismatch and Marcus's vehicle delay. (b) linear_issue_f85be674c9b8, "Document BrightLoop ops gaps: Marcus vendor miss, Simone housing trace, Priya ADA handling, Oliver UK workflow", priority 2, assigned to Chloe Vance.
OE 19: Look up Jae-won Kim's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Jae-won" or "Kim" or similar). Expected finding: email jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io, city Austin, description notes temporary corporate housing with permanent lease paperwork unsigned.
OE 20: Look up Julian Brooks's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Julian" or "Brooks" or similar). Expected finding: email julian.brooks@moveops.com.
OE 21: Send email to Jae-won Kim from Omar using send_email (sender: omar.ibrahim@moveops.com, recipients: [jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io]). Subject should reference the Austin lease or housing situation. Content should: (a) acknowledge that Omar personally handled his request and let it sit, (b) explain that the housing paperwork was incorrectly treated as complete while the lease was still pending guarantor documentation, (c) confirm the team is actively working on resolving the lease, (d) take responsibility for the support delay.
OE 22: Add a comment to a Jae-won Kim / StormCloud Linear issue (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 or linear_issue_a49b4d199139) using linear_create_comment. Content should document: (a) the low-priority classification was based on Airtable showing Completed/Confirmed when the lease was actually unsigned, (b) the relocation ticket had been closed prematurely despite outstanding guarantor docs, (c) current recovery status.
OE 23: Log a CRM engagement on the StormCloud account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: [company_stormcloud]). Content should cover: (a) Liam Park's escalation citing four unresolved issues, (b) internal reconstruction findings, Omar's triage error, premature ticket closure, Airtable status mismatch, credit misapplication, (c) recovery actions in motion.
OE 24: Post a summary in #customer-support (channel C007) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C007). Content must cover all three situations: (a) StormCloud: CFO escalation, Jae-won's unsigned lease, Omar's triage error, $500 credit misapplication, Simone Richter's frozen stipend, dropped Ridgeline referral, Mina's recovery in motion. (b) NorthWind: Julian's logged Emilia Cruz piano damage call with unanswered callback, Pam Kowalski's final warning with PathFinder competitor threat, David leading retention response. (c) BrightLoop: Simone Richter apartment mismatch (studio vs promised 1BR), Marcus Webb vehicle stuck at Road Runner Indianapolis hub, Julian already sent recovery replies to both employees, underlying issues still open.
OE 25: Send email to Julian Brooks from Omar using send_email (sender: omar.ibrahim@moveops.com, recipients: [julian.brooks@moveops.com]). Content should be a full rundown covering all three situations: (a) StormCloud: identified as the CFO-complaint client, chain of failures, Omar's triage error, Mina's recovery plan. (b) NorthWind: identified Julian's Emilia Cruz call, unanswered callback, Pam's final warning, PathFinder threat. (c) BrightLoop: Simone apartment mismatch status, Marcus vehicle delay status, Julian's recovery actions confirmed but root issues still open. This prepares Julian for David's questions.
